title: Test the New Process
description: Verify that a custom change process functions correctly. Create change objects, follow the workflow, and validate that life cycle states progress as expected.

topics.title: Verifying a Custom Change Process

content.text: After configuring a custom change process - including custom subtypes, workflows, life cycles, and object initialization rules - systematic testing is critical before deploying to production. Testing verifies that every piece of the puzzle connects correctly: the right workflow launches, tasks route to the right people, and the change object progresses through all life cycle states to completion.

rows: Can you create the custom change object (e.g., Delta Review)?
The custom subtype was created correctly in Type and Attribute Management and is available in the UI.
Are workflow tasks assigned to the proper roles?
The workflow template routes activities to the correct team roles defined in the team template.
Do tasks appear in the correct user's My Tasks table?
Group membership and role assignments are configured correctly so users see their assigned work.
Does the correct Change Notice template appear when creating a Change Notice?
Template preferences and association rules are set up properly for the custom change type.
Does the correct task form template appear by default?
Task form template preferences are configured at the appropriate context level.
Does the workflow progress through all activities to completion?
The workflow routing logic, approval gates, and conditional branches function as designed.
Does the change object move into the final state (e.g., Resolved)?
The life cycle template, OIR, and workflow are properly connected to drive the object to its terminal state.

content.text: For easier testing, place a single test user into all required groups and roles. In the Delta Drills scenario, Mike Jones was added to every group so that one person could walk through the entire workflow. In production, multiple team members in different roles would handle different tasks.

Testing the Delta Review process means creating a real change object and following it through every workflow step. The complete test sequence is: Create a Delta Review, submit it, assign reviewers, perform the review, handle the decision gate (Yes/No routing), create a Delta Change Notice, complete the change tasks in sequence, audit the change notice, and verify both the Change Notice and Delta Review reach the Resolved state. Only when every step executes as expected is the custom process ready for deployment.

content.prompt: Arrange the steps for testing the Delta Review process in the correct order:
items: Create and submit a Delta Review
Verify Delta Review reaches Resolved state

keyTakeaways: Systematic testing after configuration verifies that custom subtypes, workflows, life cycles, and OIRs are all connected correctly
A verification checklist should confirm object creation, task routing, template availability, workflow progression, and final state transitions
Using a single test user in all roles simplifies testing by allowing one person to walk through the entire workflow
The process is not ready for deployment until every step executes as expected and the change object reaches its terminal state

topics.title: Course Summary and Best Practices

content.text: This course guided you through the complete journey of tailoring Windchill change management - from understanding the building blocks to planning, implementing, and testing a custom change process. The Delta Drills scenario demonstrated every major step using a real-world regulatory review requirement. As you apply these techniques in your own organization, the best practices below will help you avoid common pitfalls and build processes that scale.

cards.back: Identified the six change objects and six administrative elements that form the foundation of Windchill change management. Explored the OOTB five-step change process, the Fast Track vs. Full Track options, and the three Change Administrator roles.

Created custom change objects and user-defined groups using Type and Attribute Management. Configured change management preferences, task form templates, association rules, mapping rules, and change intent to support the Delta Drills scenario.

Assessed, modified, and created workflow templates and life cycle templates. Built Object Initialization Rules to connect workflows with life cycles, ensuring the custom process launches automatically when a Delta Review is created.

Verified the complete Delta Review process by creating a change object, following the workflow through every step, and confirming the object reaches the Resolved state. Validated that all configuration pieces work together as designed.

content.text: The three pillars of change process configuration are Workflow, Life Cycle, and Object Initialization Rule (OIR). The workflow defines what happens, the life cycle defines what states the object passes through, and the OIR connects them by specifying which workflow launches at which state. Master these three elements and you can tailor any change process.
Best Practices for Tailoring Change Management

content.prompt: Match each best practice to the rationale behind it:

pairs.left: Always copy templates before modifying them
pairs.right: Preserves the original as a reference and prevents upgrade conflicts

pairs.left: Create and manage OIRs at the organization level
pairs.right: Ensures rules apply consistently across all contexts and products

pairs.left: Use the 9-step planning process before making changes
pairs.right: Prevents over-engineering by starting from actual business requirements

pairs.left: Test with a single user in all roles before deploying
pairs.right: Allows one person to walk through the complete workflow and catch issues early

content.text: Additional best practices to keep in mind: never modify original OOTB templates directly, consider turning off OOTB rules and defining custom rules when the defaults do not match your needs, and always plan for future scalability when designing custom processes. A well-planned process is far easier to maintain and extend than one built incrementally without a strategy.

keyTakeaways: The course covered four areas: understanding building blocks, planning the change strategy, tailoring workflows and life cycles, and testing the complete process
Workflow, Life Cycle, and OIR are the three pillars of change process configuration - mastering them enables you to tailor any change process
Key best practices include copying templates before modifying, managing OIRs at the organization level, following the 9-step planning process, and testing thoroughly before deployment
Always plan for future scalability and avoid modifying OOTB templates directly to ensure smooth upgrades

topics.title: Start the Delta Review Process

content.text: In this exercise, you will create a Delta Review object, submit it into the workflow, and navigate through the initial tasks - from reviewer assignment through the decision gate - to reach the Change Notice creation step. This validates that the custom subtype, workflow, and task routing you configured in previous modules all function correctly.

This exercise assumes you have completed all configuration from Modules 2 and 3, including creating the Delta Review subtype, custom groups, workflow template, life cycle template, and Object Initialization Rules. Your test user (Mike Jones) should be a member of all required groups.

content.title: Start the Delta Review Process
content.objective: Create a Delta Review object, submit it, and navigate through the initial workflow tasks to the Change Notice creation step.

steps.action: Search for HANDLE_SIDE_1000 Windchill Part and access the Information page.
steps.detail: This is the part that needs regulatory review in the Delta Drills scenario.
steps.hint: You should be signed in to Windchill as mjones/ptc.

steps.detail: The Delta Review subtype should be available because of the custom type you created in Module 2.
steps.hint: Use the Actions menu or the New action to create the Delta Review.

steps.action: In the Name field, type Testing the Delta Review Process.
steps.detail: A descriptive name helps identify this test instance during the verification process.
Categorizes the review object.
Creates the Delta Review object with the specified name and category.
Immediately submits the Delta Review into the workflow rather than saving as a draft. The Delta Regulation Review Workflow begins.
Returns to the home page where workflow tasks appear in the My Tasks table.
steps.hint: You included Mike Jones in all Delta groups to make this demonstration easier. In a real-life change process, these tasks would be completed by several people.

steps.detail: Opens the Assign Reviewers task in the workflow.
steps.hint: If a link does not appear in the My Tasks table, wait a few seconds and refresh the screen. If a ptc-training.ptc.com:8181 Attention dialog appears, click OK and continue.

steps.detail: Completes the reviewer assignment step of the workflow.

steps.action: Press Ctrl+R to refresh the home screen.
steps.detail: Refreshes the My Tasks table to show the next available task.
Opens the Perform Review task, which represents the entire review process.
Completes the review step of the workflow.

steps.action: Press Ctrl+R to refresh the home screen.
steps.detail: Refreshes the My Tasks table to show the next available task.
Opens the modified approval activity where you choose Yes or No on whether the regulation requires a change.
Switches to the tab that contains workflow process actions.
Opens the task completion controls.
Completes the Decision Required task with the default (Yes) routing, advancing the workflow to the Change Notice creation step.

steps.action: Press Ctrl+R to refresh the home screen.
steps.detail: Refreshes the My Tasks table to show the Create Delta Change Notice task.
Opens the Create Delta Change Notice task from the My Tasks table.

steps.action: Click the Delta Regulation Review Workflow link next to the Process attribute to open the Process Manager in a new tab.
steps.detail: The Process Manager shows a visual representation of the workflow progress.

steps.action: Enlarge the upper field to see the entire workflow.
steps.detail: Expanding the view lets you see all workflow nodes and their status.

steps.action: Verify the Create Delta Change Notice activity icon is green and states that it is Running. Note that prior activities are marked as Executed.
steps.detail: Confirms the workflow has progressed correctly through all prior steps to the Change Notice creation point.

steps.action: Return to the Task web browser tab.
steps.detail: Switches back to the task page to continue with the workflow.
Switches to the tab containing workflow actions.
Opens the list of actions available for the subject object.
Initiates creating a Change Notice associated with the Delta Review. This is possible because of the association rule you created in Exercise 5.
Carries forward relevant data from the Delta Review to the new Change Notice.

steps.action: Select Delta Change Notice in the Template field.
steps.detail: Uses the Delta Change Notice template for creating the change notice.
Advances to the task configuration step where you assign tasks.

steps.action: Note that the Assignee and Reviewer have not been designated for either task.
steps.detail: The task assignments need to be configured manually at this point.

steps.action: Click the Edit change task icon for the Delta Change Task.
steps.detail: Opens the task editor for the first change task.
Disables the review requirement for this task during testing.
Begins searching for the user to assign to this task.

steps.action: Select Jones, Mike from the pop-up line.
steps.detail: Assigns Mike Jones as the task assignee.
Saves the Delta Change Task configuration.

steps.action: Click the Edit change task icon for the Delta Regulatory Task.
steps.detail: Opens the task editor for the second change task.
Disables the review requirement for this task during testing.
Begins searching for the user to assign to this task.

steps.action: Select Jones, Mike from the pop-up line.
steps.detail: Assigns Mike Jones as the task assignee.
Saves the Delta Regulatory Task configuration.
steps.hint: You can specify the order in which the tasks will be completed because of the preference set in Exercise 3.

steps.detail: Completes the Change Notice creation wizard.
Submits the Delta Change Notice into its workflow.

steps.action: Select Task Completion on the Delta Regulation Review Workflow – Create Delta Change Notice Task page.
steps.detail: Opens the task completion controls for the Create Delta Change Notice task.
Completes the Create Delta Change Notice task, advancing the Delta Review workflow.

steps.action: Select the Process Manager web browser tab.
steps.detail: Switches to the Process Manager to verify workflow progress.
Refreshes the Process Manager view to show the latest workflow state.

steps.action: Verify the Synch on Delta Change Notice Completion robot is now green.
steps.detail: Confirms that the workflow has progressed past the Change Notice creation step.

steps.action: Select the Windchill – Home web browser tab.
steps.detail: Returns to the home page to continue with the remaining tasks.

keyTakeaways: Creating a real change object and submitting it is the most effective way to validate your custom configuration
Workflow tasks appear in the My Tasks table and must be completed in sequence to advance the process
The Process Manager provides a visual representation of workflow progress, showing which activities are running or completed
Association rules and template preferences control which Change Notice templates and task configurations are available during the process

topics.title: Complete and Verify the Process

content.text: In this exercise, you will complete the remaining change tasks, finish the audit step, and verify that both the Delta Change Notice and Delta Review reach the Resolved state. This confirms that the entire custom change process - from creation to completion - functions as designed.

content.title: Complete and Verify the Process
content.objective: Complete the remaining change tasks, finish the workflow, and verify the Delta Review reaches the Resolved state.

steps.action: Click the Complete Change Notice Task text next to the Delta Regulatory Task.
steps.detail: Opens the first Change Notice task. The regulatory task appears first because of the sequenced plan.
Opens the task completion controls.
Marks the Delta Regulatory Task as done.

steps.action: Press Ctrl+R to refresh the home screen.
steps.detail: Refreshes the My Tasks table to show the next available task.

steps.action: Click the Complete Change Notice Task text next to the Delta Change Task.
steps.detail: Opens the second Change Notice task. The change task becomes available after the regulatory task is completed.
Opens the task completion controls.
Marks the Delta Change Task as done. Both change tasks are now complete.

steps.action: Press Ctrl+R to refresh the home screen.
steps.detail: Refreshes the My Tasks table to show the audit task.
Opens the Audit Change Notice task, which is the final review step before the change process completes.
Opens the task completion controls for the audit task.

steps.action: Ensure the radio button for Complete is selected.
steps.detail: Selecting Complete allows the workflow to finish successfully. Selecting Rework would send the process back.
Completes the audit, allowing the workflow to proceed to its final state.

steps.action: Press Ctrl+R to refresh the home screen.
steps.detail: Refreshes the My Tasks table.

steps.action: Verify there are no new tasks.
steps.detail: No remaining tasks indicates the workflow has completed all steps.

tasks.title: Check That Delta Review Is Resolved

steps.action: Click the Updates link on Mike Jones' Home page.
steps.detail: The Updates section shows recently changed objects and their current status.
Refreshes the Updates list to show the latest status of all objects.

steps.action: Verify that Change Notice: Testing the Delta Review Process is listed as Resolved.
steps.detail: The Change Notice reaching Resolved state confirms the CN workflow completed successfully.

steps.action: Verify that Delta Review: Testing the Delta Review Process is listed as Resolved.
steps.detail: The Delta Review reaching Resolved state confirms the entire custom process completed successfully.

steps.action: Select the Process Manager web browser tab.
steps.detail: Switches to the Process Manager for a visual verification of the workflow.
Refreshes the Process Manager to show the final workflow state.

steps.action: Note that all steps in the upper Resolve branch after the Synch on Delta Change Notice Completion robot have been executed.
steps.detail: Confirms that every step in the workflow was completed in the correct order.
Closes the Process Manager. The verification is complete – the Delta Drills custom change process is functioning as designed.

keyTakeaways: Change tasks execute in sequence when the Sequenced Plan preference is enabled, ensuring tasks are completed in the correct order
The audit step is the final gate before the change process completes - selecting "Complete" allows the workflow to finish
Both the Change Notice and Delta Review must reach the Resolved state to confirm the entire process completed successfully
The Process Manager provides visual confirmation that every workflow step executed in the correct order
